content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

July 27, 1995

Carolyn Johnson
Community Development Department
City of Pismo Beach
Post Office Box 3
760 Mattie Road
Pismo Beach, California 93449

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-219

Dear Ms. Johnson:

You have requested advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ as they
pertain to your position as a city planner for the city of Pismo
Beach.

QUESTION

May you participate in decisions regarding transactions
between the City of Pismo Beach and the Peoples Self Help Housing
("PSHH") nonprofit corporation, since you serve as a non-salaried
member of the Board of Directors of PSHH?

CONCLUSION

As a non-salaried member of the Board of Directors of PSHH,
you do not have a financial interest in PSHH. Therefore, you do
not have a conflict of interest under the Act.

However, other provisions may apply to your situation; please
refer to your statement of incompatible activities to ascertain if
there is a problem with being on the board of PSHH relative to
your position as a city planner.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18995.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-95-219
Page 2


FACTS

You are employed as a city planner for the City of Pismo
Beach and you are also on the board of directors for a nonprofit
housing corporation, Peoples Self Help Housing. You do not
receive any salary or per diem from PSHH.

PSHH is pursuing the development of a residential facility in
the City of Pismo Beach. As a planner for the city, you are
charged with providing information related to development
applications.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participate in making, or using his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he or she knows or has
reason to know he or she has a financial interest. An official
has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally,
on the official or a member of his or her immediate family, or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

Section 87103.

File No. A-95-219
Page 3


     As a public official (Section 82048), you may not make,
participate in making, or in any way attempt to influence a
decision in which you have a financial interest. (Section 87100.)

     Since PSHH is a nonprofit organization, it is not a "business
entity" as defined in Section 82005. Thus, PSHH is not a
disqualifying financial interest for purposes of Sections 87103(a)
and (d). (Eymil Advice Letter, No. I-93-177.) Furthermore, PSHH
is not a source of income to you since you serve as a non-salaried
member of the board of directors. Therefore, unless you are
personally affected, as provided by Regulation 18702.1(a)(4)2, you
do not have a financial interest which may be affected by any
decisions regarding transactions between the City of Pismo Beach
and PSHH.

     Our advice to you is limited to the provisions of the
Political Reform Act. Please review the statement of incompatible
activities for city planners to determine whether other provisions
apply to your situation.

     I trust this answers your question.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Steven G. Churchwell
                                   General Counsel

                                   By: Jill Stecher
                                       Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak


_______________________
2  Regulation 18702.1(a)(4) provides that an official may not
participate in a decision if it will result in the personal
expenses, income, assets (other than interest in real property),
or liabilities of the official or his or her immediate family
increasing or decreasing by at least $250.
